headline: Ride-Hailing Drivers Average RM6.20 Per Hour After Costs
context: A University Malaya study found that Grab and Maxim drivers in Kuala Lumpur earned an effective hourly rate of RM6.20 after deducting fuel, vehicle wear, platform commissions, and insurance — below the RM8.65 minimum wage equivalent.

cards.big: RM6.20 per hour after costs — Grab drivers earn 28% below minimum wage while the platform takes 25% commission
cards.sub: Drivers work average 11 hours daily to reach RM68 net income — below RM1,700 minimum wage on a per-hour basis

cards.big: Grab Malaysia's 2025 revenue of RM3.8 billion generated RM640 million in profit — drivers absorbed RM4.2 billion in costs
cards.sub: Platform retains 25% commission plus RM0.30 booking fee — effective take rate is 32% of gross fare

cards.big: 148,000 active ride-hailing drivers — 38% are former private sector employees who lost jobs during pandemic and never returned
cards.sub: No sick leave, no annual leave, no EPF — drivers bear 100% of income risk while platforms bear zero employment liability

cards.big: Transport Ministry classifies ride-hailing drivers as independent contractors — exempting platforms from Employment Act obligations
cards.sub: APAD regulates vehicle standards but has no mandate over driver earnings or working conditions

cards.big: When a full-time job pays below minimum wage but the law says it's not employment, the law protects the platform — not the person doing the work
Should ride-hailing platforms be required to guarantee minimum hourly earnings of RM10 after costs for active drivers?
